NKJV.1: The words of King Lemuel, the utterance which his mother taught him:
NKJV.2: What, my son? And what, son of my womb? And what, son of my vows?
NKJV.3: Do not give your strength to women, Nor your ways to that which destroys kings.
NKJV.4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, It is not for kings to drink wine, Nor for princes intoxicating drink;
NKJV.5: Lest they drink and forget the law, And pervert the justice of all the afflicted.
NKJV.6: Give strong drink to him who is perishing, And wine to those who are bitter of heart.
NKJV.7: Let him drink and forget his poverty, And remember his misery no more.
NKJV.8: Open your mouth for the speechless, In the cause of all who are appointed to die.
NKJV.9: Open your mouth, judge righteously, And plead the cause of the poor and needy.
NKJV.10: Who can find a virtuous wife? For her worth is far above rubies.
NKJV.11: The heart of her husband safely trusts her; So he will have no lack of gain.
NKJV.12: She does him good and not evil All the days of her life.
NKJV.13: She seeks wool and flax, And willingly works with her hands.
NKJV.14: She is like the merchant ships, She brings her food from afar.
NKJV.15: She also rises while it is yet night, And provides food for her household, And a portion for her maidservants.
NKJV.16: She considers a field and buys it; From her profits she plants a vineyard.
NKJV.17: She girds herself with strength, And strengthens her arms.
NKJV.18: She perceives that her merchandise is good, And her lamp does not go out by night.
NKJV.19: She stretches out her hands to the distaff, And her hand holds the spindle.
NKJV.20: She extends her hand to the poor, Yes, she reaches out her hands to the needy.
NKJV.21: She is not afraid of snow for her household, For all her household is clothed with scarlet.
NKJV.22: She makes tapestry for herself; Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
NKJV.23: Her husband is known in the gates, When he sits among the elders of the land.
NKJV.24: She makes linen garments and sells them, And supplies sashes for the merchants.
NKJV.25: Strength and honor are her clothing; She shall rejoice in time to come.
NKJV.26: She opens her mouth with wisdom, And on her tongue is the law of kindness.
NKJV.27: She watches over the ways of her household, And does not eat the bread of idleness.
NKJV.28: Her children rise up and call her blessed; Her husband also, and he praises her:
NKJV.29: “Many daughters have done well, But you excel them all.”
NKJV.30: Charm is deceitful and beauty is passing, But a woman who fears the LORD, she shall be praised.
NKJV.31: Give her of the fruit of her hands, And let her own works praise her in the gates.

ESV.1: The words of King Lemuel. An oracle that his mother taught him:
ESV.2: What are you doing, my son?  What are you doing, son of my womb? What are you doing, son of my vows?
ESV.3: Do not give your strength to women, your ways to those who destroy kings.
ESV.4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, or for rulers to take strong drink,
ESV.5: lest they drink and forget what has been decreed and pervert the rights of all the afflicted.
ESV.6: Give strong drink to the one who is perishing, and wine to those in bitter distress;
ESV.7: let them drink and forget their poverty and remember their misery no more.
ESV.8: Open your mouth for the mute, for the rights of all who are destitute.
ESV.9: Open your mouth, judge righteously, defend the rights of the poor and needy.
ESV.10: An excellent wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels.
ESV.11: The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he will have no lack of gain.
ESV.12: She does him good, and not harm, all the days of her life.
ESV.13: She seeks wool and flax, and works with willing hands.
ESV.14: She is like the ships of the merchant; she brings her food from afar.
ESV.15: She rises while it is yet night and provides food for her household and portions for her maidens.
ESV.16: She considers a field and buys it; with the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
ESV.17: She dresses herself  with strength and makes her arms strong.
ESV.18: She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp does not go out at night.
ESV.19: She puts her hands to the distaff, and her hands hold the spindle.
ESV.20: She opens her hand to the poor and reaches out her hands to the needy.
ESV.21: She is not afraid of snow for her household, for all her household are clothed in scarlet.
ESV.22: She makes bed coverings for herself; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
ESV.23: Her husband is known in the gates when he sits among the elders of the land.
ESV.24: She makes linen garments and sells them; she delivers sashes to the merchant.
ESV.25: Strength and dignity are her clothing, and she laughs at the time to come.
ESV.26: She opens her mouth with wisdom, and the teaching of kindness is on her tongue.
ESV.27: She looks well to the ways of her household and does not eat the bread of idleness.
ESV.28: Her children rise up and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praises her:
ESV.29: “Many women have done excellently, but you surpass them all.”
ESV.30: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.
ESV.31: Give her of the fruit of her hands, and let her works praise her in the gates.

NASB.1: The words of King Lemuel, the oracle which his mother taught him:
NASB.2: What, O my son? And what, O son of my womb? And what, O son of my vows?
NASB.3: Do not give your strength to women, Or your ways to that which destroys kings.
NASB.4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, It is not for kings to drink wine, Or for rulers to desire strong drink,
NASB.5: For they will drink and forget what is decreed, And pervert the rights of all the afflicted.
NASB.6: Give strong drink to him who is perishing, And wine to him whose life is bitter.
NASB.7: Let him drink and forget his poverty And remember his trouble no more.
NASB.8: Open your mouth for the mute, For the rights of all the unfortunate.
NASB.9: Open your mouth, judge righteously, And defend the rights of the afflicted and needy.
NASB.10: An excellent wife, who can find? For her worth is far above jewels.
NASB.11: The heart of her husband trusts in her, And he will have no lack of gain.
NASB.12: She does him good and not evil All the days of her life.
NASB.13: She looks for wool and flax And works with her hands in delight.
NASB.14: She is like merchant ships; She brings her food from afar.
NASB.15: She rises also while it is still night And gives food to her household And portions to her maidens.
NASB.16: She considers a field and buys it; From her earnings she plants a vineyard.
NASB.17: She girds herself with strength And makes her arms strong.
NASB.18: She senses that her gain is good; Her lamp does not go out at night.
NASB.19: She stretches out her hands to the distaff, And her hands grasp the spindle.
NASB.20: She extends her hand to the poor, And she stretches out her hands to the needy.
NASB.21: She is not afraid of the snow for her household, For all her household are clothed with scarlet.
NASB.22: She makes coverings for herself; Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
NASB.23: Her husband is known in the gates, When he sits among the elders of the land.
NASB.24: She makes linen garments and sells [them], And supplies belts to the tradesmen.
NASB.25: Strength and dignity are her clothing, And she smiles at the future.
NASB.26: She opens her mouth in wisdom, And the teaching of kindness is on her tongue.
NASB.27: She looks well to the ways of her household, And does not eat the bread of idleness.
NASB.28: Her children rise up and bless her; Her husband [also], and he praises her, [saying]:
NASB.29: 'Many daughters have done nobly, But you excel them all.'
NASB.30: Charm is deceitful and beauty is vain, [But] a woman who fears the LORD, she shall be praised.
NASB.31: Give her the product of her hands, And let her works praise her in the gates.

NLT.1: The sayings of King Lemuel contain this message, which his mother taught him.
NLT.2:
O my son, O son of my womb,
O son of my vows,
NLT.3:
do not waste your strength on women,
on those who ruin kings.
NLT.4:
It is not for kings, O Lemuel, to guzzle wine.
Rulers should not crave alcohol.
NLT.5:
For if they drink, they may forget the law
and not give justice to the oppressed.
NLT.6:
Alcohol is for the dying,
and wine for those in bitter distress.
NLT.7:
Let them drink to forget their poverty
and remember their troubles no more.
NLT.8:
Speak up for those who cannot speak for themselves;
ensure justice for those being crushed.
NLT.9:
Yes, speak up for the poor and helpless,
and see that they get justice.
NLT.10:
Who can find a virtuous and capable wife?
She is more precious than rubies.
NLT.11:
Her husband can trust her,
and she will greatly enrich his life.
NLT.12:
She brings him good, not harm,
all the days of her life.
NLT.13:
She finds wool and flax
and busily spins it.
NLT.14:
She is like a merchant’s ship,
bringing her food from afar.
NLT.15:
She gets up before dawn to prepare breakfast for her household
and plan the day’s work for her servant girls.
NLT.16:
She goes to inspect a field and buys it;
with her earnings she plants a vineyard.
NLT.17:
She is energetic and strong,
a hard worker.
NLT.18:
She makes sure her dealings are profitable;
her lamp burns late into the night.
NLT.19:
Her hands are busy spinning thread,
her fingers twisting fiber.
NLT.20:
She extends a helping hand to the poor
and opens her arms to the needy.
NLT.21:
She has no fear of winter for her household,
for everyone has warm clothes.
NLT.22:
She makes her own bedspreads.
She dresses in fine linen and purple gowns.
NLT.23:
Her husband is well known at the city gates,
where he sits with the other civic leaders.
NLT.24:
She makes belted linen garments
and sashes to sell to the merchants.
NLT.25:
She is clothed with strength and dignity,
and she laughs without fear of the future.
NLT.26:
When she speaks, her words are wise,
and she gives instructions with kindness.
NLT.27:
She carefully watches everything in her household
and suffers nothing from laziness.
NLT.28:
Her children stand and bless her.
Her husband praises her:
NLT.29:
“There are many virtuous and capable women in the world,
but you surpass them all!”
NLT.30:
Charm is deceptive, and beauty does not last;
but a woman who fears the LORD will be greatly praised.
NLT.31:
Reward her for all she has done.
Let her deeds publicly declare her praise.

WEB.1: The words of king Lemuel; the revelation which his mother taught him.
WEB.2: “Oh, my son! Oh, son of my womb! Oh, son of my vows!
WEB.3: Don’t give your strength to women, nor your ways to that which destroys kings.
WEB.4: It is not for kings, Lemuel; it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes to say, ‘Where is strong drink?’
WEB.5: lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the justice due to anyone who is afflicted.
WEB.6: Give strong drink to him who is ready to perish; and wine to the bitter in soul:
WEB.7: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.
WEB.8: Open your mouth for the mute, in the cause of all who are left desolate.
WEB.9: Open your mouth, judge righteously, and serve justice to the poor and needy.”
WEB.10: Who can find a worthy woman? For her price is far above rubies.
WEB.11: The heart of her husband trusts in her. He shall have no lack of gain.
WEB.12: She does him good, and not harm, all the days of her life.
WEB.13: She seeks wool and flax, and works eagerly with her hands.
WEB.14: She is like the merchant ships. She brings her bread from afar.
WEB.15: She rises also while it is yet night, gives food to her household, and portions for her servant girls.
WEB.16: She considers a field, and buys it. With the fruit of her hands, she plants a vineyard.
WEB.17: She arms her waist with strength, and makes her arms strong.
WEB.18: She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp doesn’t go out by night.
WEB.19: She lays her hands to the distaff, and her hands hold the spindle.
WEB.20: She opens her arms to the poor; yes, she extends her hands to the needy.
WEB.21: She is not afraid of the snow for her household; for all her household are clothed with scarlet.
WEB.22: She makes for herself carpets of tapestry. Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
WEB.23: Her husband is respected in the gates, when he sits among the elders of the land.
WEB.24: She makes linen garments and sells them, and delivers sashes to the merchant.
WEB.25: Strength and dignity are her clothing. She laughs at the time to come.
WEB.26: She opens her mouth with wisdom. Faithful instruction is on her tongue.
WEB.27: She looks well to the ways of her household, and doesn’t eat the bread of idleness.
WEB.28: Her children rise up and call her blessed. Her husband also praises her:
WEB.29: “Many women do noble things, but you excel them all.”
WEB.30: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain; but a woman who fears Yahweh, she shall be praised.
WEB.31: Give her of the fruit of her hands! Let her works praise her in the gates!

CSB17.1:
The words of King Lemuel,
a pronouncement [1] ⓐ that his mother taught him:
CSB17.2:
What should I say, my son?
What, son of my womb? ⓑ
What, son of my vows?
CSB17.3:
Don’t spend your energy ⓒ on women
or your efforts on those who destroy kings. ⓓ
CSB17.4:
It is not for kings, Lemuel,
it is not for kings ⓔ to drink wine ⓕ
or for rulers to desire beer.
CSB17.5:
Otherwise, he will drink,
forget what is decreed, ⓖ
and pervert justice for all the oppressed. [2] ⓗ
CSB17.6:
Give beer to one who is dying
and wine to one whose life is bitter. ⓘ
CSB17.7:
Let him drink so that he can forget his poverty
and remember his trouble no more. ⓙ
CSB17.8:
Speak up [3] for those who have no voice, [4] ⓚ
for the justice of all who are dispossessed. [5]
CSB17.9:
Speak up, judge righteously, ⓛ
and defend the cause of the oppressed and needy. ⓜ
CSB17.10:
Who can find a wife of noble character? [6] ⓝ
She is far more precious than jewels. [7]
CSB17.11:
The heart of her husband trusts in her,
and he will not lack anything good.
CSB17.12:
She rewards him with good, not evil,
all the days of her life.
CSB17.13:
She selects wool and flax
and works with willing hands.
CSB17.14:
She is like the merchant ships,
bringing her food from far away. ⓐ
CSB17.15:
She rises while it is still night ⓑ
and provides food for her household
and portions [8] for her female servants. ⓒ
CSB17.16:
She evaluates a field and buys it;
she plants a vineyard with her earnings. [9] ⓓ
CSB17.17:
She draws on her strength [10]
and reveals that her arms are strong. ⓔ
CSB17.18:
She sees that her profits are good,
and her lamp never goes out at night. ⓕ
CSB17.19:
She extends her hands to the spinning staff,
and her hands hold the spindle. ⓖ
CSB17.20:
Her hands reach [11] out to the poor,
and she extends her hands to the needy. ⓗ
CSB17.21:
She is not afraid for her household when it snows,
for all in her household are doubly clothed. [12]
CSB17.22:
She makes her own bed coverings; ⓘ
her clothing is fine linen ⓙ and purple. ⓚ
CSB17.23:
Her husband is known at the city gates,
where he sits among the elders of the land. ⓛ
CSB17.24:
She makes and sells linen garments;
she delivers belts [13] to the merchants.
CSB17.25:
Strength ⓜ and honor are her clothing, ⓝ
and she can laugh at the time to come. ⓐ
CSB17.26:
Her mouth speaks wisdom,
and loving instruction [14] is on her tongue.
CSB17.27:
She watches over the activities of her household ⓑ
and is never idle. [15]
CSB17.28:
Her children rise up and call her blessed;
her husband also praises her:
CSB17.29:
“Many women [16] have done noble deeds,
but you surpass them all!” ⓒ
CSB17.30:
Charm is deceptive and beauty is fleeting, ⓓ
but a woman who fears the LORD will be praised.
CSB17.31:
Give her the reward of her labor, [17]
and let her works praise her at the city gates.

AMP.1: The words of King Lemuel, the oracle, which his mother taught him:
AMP.2:
What, O my son?
And what, O son of my womb?
And what [shall I advise you], O son of my vows?
AMP.3:
Do not give your [generative] strength to women [neither foreign wives in marriages of alliances, nor concubines],
Nor your ways to that which destroys kings.
AMP.4:
It is not for kings, O Lemuel,
It is not for kings to drink wine,
Or for rulers to desire strong drink,
AMP.5:
Otherwise they drink and forget the law and its decrees,
And pervert the rights and justice of all the afflicted.
AMP.6:
Give strong drink [as medicine] to him who is ready to pass away,
And wine to him whose life is bitter.
AMP.7:
Let him drink and forget his poverty
And no longer remember his trouble.
AMP.8:
Open your mouth for the mute,
For the rights of all who are unfortunate and defenseless;
AMP.9:
Open your mouth, judge righteously,
And administer justice for the afflicted and needy.
AMP.10:
An excellent woman [one who is spiritual, capable, intelligent, and virtuous], who is he who can find her?
Her value is more precious than jewels and her worth is far above rubies or pearls.
AMP.11:
The heart of her husband trusts in her [with secure confidence],
And he will have no lack of gain.
AMP.12:
She comforts, encourages, and does him only good and not evil
All the days of her life.
AMP.13:
She looks for wool and flax
And works with willing hands in delight.
AMP.14:
She is like the merchant ships [abounding with treasure];
She brings her [household's] food from far away.
AMP.15:
She rises also while it is still night
And gives food to her household
And assigns tasks to her maids.
AMP.16:
She considers a field before she buys or accepts it [expanding her business prudently];
With her profits she plants fruitful vines in her vineyard.
AMP.17:
She equips herself with strength [spiritual, mental, and physical fitness for her God-given task]
And makes her arms strong.
AMP.18:
She sees that her gain is good;
Her lamp does not go out, but it burns continually through the night [she is prepared for whatever lies ahead].
AMP.19:
She stretches out her hands to the distaff,
And her hands hold the spindle [as she spins wool into thread for clothing].
AMP.20:
She opens and extends her hand to the poor,
And she reaches out her filled hands to the needy.
AMP.21:
She does not fear the snow for her household,
For all in her household are clothed in [expensive] scarlet [wool].
AMP.22:
She makes for herself coverlets, cushions, and rugs of tapestry.
Her clothing is linen, pure and fine, and purple [wool].
AMP.23:
Her husband is known in the [city's] gates,
When he sits among the elders of the land.
AMP.24:
She makes [fine] linen garments and sells them;
And supplies sashes to the merchants.
AMP.25:
Strength and dignity are her clothing and her position is strong and secure;
And she smiles at the future [knowing that she and her family are prepared].
AMP.26:
She opens her mouth in [skillful and godly] wisdom,
And the teaching of kindness is on her tongue [giving counsel and instruction].
AMP.27:
She looks well to how things go in her household,
And does not eat the bread of idleness.
AMP.28:
Her children rise up and call her blessed (happy, prosperous, to be admired);
Her husband also, and he praises her, saying,
AMP.29:
"Many daughters have done nobly, and well [with the strength of character that is steadfast in goodness],
But you excel them all."
AMP.30:
Charm and grace are deceptive, and [superficial] beauty is vain,
But a woman who fears the LORD [reverently worshiping, obeying, serving, and trusting Him with awe-filled respect], she shall be praised.
AMP.31:
Give her of the product of her hands,
And let her own works praise her in the gates [of the city].

MSG.1:
The words of King Lemuel,
the strong advice his mother gave him:
MSG.2:
"Oh, son of mine, what can you be thinking of!
Child whom I bore! The son I dedicated to God!
MSG.3:
Don't dissipate your virility on fortune-hunting women,
promiscuous women who shipwreck leaders.
MSG.4:
"Leaders can't afford to make fools of themselves,
gulping wine and swilling beer,
MSG.5:
Lest, hung over, they don't know right from wrong,
and the people who depend on them are hurt.
MSG.6:
Use wine and beer only as sedatives,
to kill the pain and dull the ache
MSG.7:
Of the terminally ill,
for whom life is a living death.
MSG.8:
"Speak up for the people who have no voice,
for the rights of all the down-and-outers.
MSG.9:
Speak out for justice!
Stand up for the poor and destitute!"
MSG.10:
A good woman is hard to find,
and worth far more than diamonds.
MSG.11:
Her husband trusts her without reserve,
and never has reason to regret it.
MSG.12:
Never spiteful, she treats him generously
all her life long.
MSG.13:
She shops around for the best yarns and cottons,
and enjoys knitting and sewing.
MSG.14:
She's like a trading ship that sails to faraway places
and brings back exotic surprises.
MSG.15:
She's up before dawn, preparing breakfast
for her family and organizing her day.
MSG.16:
She looks over a field and buys it,
then, with money she's put aside, plants a garden.
MSG.17:
First thing in the morning, she dresses for work,
rolls up her sleeves, eager to get started.
MSG.18:
She senses the worth of her work,
is in no hurry to call it quits for the day.
MSG.19:
She's skilled in the crafts of home and hearth,
diligent in homemaking.
MSG.20:
She's quick to assist anyone in need,
reaches out to help the poor.
MSG.21:
She doesn't worry about her family when it snows;
their winter clothes are all mended and ready to wear.
MSG.22:
She makes her own clothing,
and dresses in colorful linens and silks.
MSG.23:
Her husband is greatly respected
when he deliberates with the city fathers.
MSG.24:
She designs gowns and sells them,
brings the sweaters she knits to the dress shops.
MSG.25:
Her clothes are well-made and elegant,
and she always faces tomorrow with a smile.
MSG.26:
When she speaks she has something worthwhile to say,
and she always says it kindly.
MSG.27:
She keeps an eye on everyone in her household,
and keeps them all busy and productive.
MSG.28:
Her children respect and bless her;
her husband joins in with words of praise:
MSG.29:
"Many women have done wonderful things,
but you've outclassed them all!"
MSG.30:
Charm can mislead and beauty soon fades.
The woman to be admired and praised
is the woman who lives in the Fear-of-God.
MSG.31:
Give her everything she deserves!
Festoon her life with praises!

NIV.1:
Sayings of King Lemuel
The sayings of King Lemuel—an oracle his mother taught him:
NIV.2:
“O my son, O son of my womb,
O son of my vows,
NIV.3:
do not spend your strength on women,
your vigor on those who ruin kings.
NIV.4:
“It is not for kings, O Lemuel—
not for kings to drink wine,
not for rulers to crave beer,
NIV.5:
lest they drink and forget what the law decrees,
and deprive all the oppressed of their rights.
NIV.6:
Give beer to those who are perishing,
wine to those who are in anguish;
NIV.7:
let them drink and forget their poverty
and remember their misery no more.
NIV.8:
“Speak up for those who cannot speak for themselves,
for the rights of all who are destitute.
NIV.9:
Speak up and judge fairly;
defend the rights of the poor and needy.”
NIV.10:
Epilogue: The Wife of Noble Character
A wife of noble character who can find?
She is worth far more than rubies.
NIV.11:
Her husband has full confidence in her
and lacks nothing of value.
NIV.12:
She brings him good, not harm,
all the days of her life.
NIV.13:
She selects wool and flax
and works with eager hands.
NIV.14:
She is like the merchant ships,
bringing her food from afar.
NIV.15:
She gets up while it is still dark;
she provides food for her family
and portions for her servant girls.
NIV.16:
She considers a field and buys it;
out of her earnings she plants a vineyard.
NIV.17:
She sets about her work vigorously;
her arms are strong for her tasks.
NIV.18:
She sees that her trading is profitable,
and her lamp does not go out at night.
NIV.19:
In her hand she holds the distaff
and grasps the spindle with her fingers.
NIV.20:
She opens her arms to the poor
and extends her hands to the needy.
NIV.21:
When it snows, she has no fear for her household;
for all of them are clothed in scarlet.
NIV.22:
She makes coverings for her bed;
she is clothed in fine linen and purple.
NIV.23:
Her husband is respected at the city gate,
where he takes his seat among the elders of the land.
NIV.24:
She makes linen garments and sells them,
and supplies the merchants with sashes.
NIV.25:
She is clothed with strength and dignity;
she can laugh at the days to come.
NIV.26:
She speaks with wisdom,
and faithful instruction is on her tongue.
NIV.27:
She watches over the affairs of her household
and does not eat the bread of idleness.
NIV.28:
Her children arise and call her blessed;
her husband also, and he praises her:
NIV.29:
“Many women do noble things,
but you surpass them all.”
NIV.30:
Charm is deceptive, and beauty is fleeting;
but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised.
NIV.31:
Give her the reward she has earned,
and let her works bring her praise at the city gate.

NRSVCE.1: The words of King Lemuel. An oracle that his mother taught him:
NRSVCE.2:
No, my son! No, son of my womb!
No, son of my vows!
NRSVCE.3:
Do not give your strength to women,
your ways to those who destroy kings.
NRSVCE.4:
It is not for kings, O Lemuel,
it is not for kings to drink wine,
or for rulers to desire strong drink;
NRSVCE.5:
or else they will drink and forget what has been decreed,
and will pervert the rights of all the afflicted.
NRSVCE.6:
Give strong drink to one who is perishing,
and wine to those in bitter distress;
NRSVCE.7:
let them drink and forget their poverty,
and remember their misery no more.
NRSVCE.8:
Speak out for those who cannot speak,
for the rights of all the destitute.
NRSVCE.9:
Speak out, judge righteously,
defend the rights of the poor and needy.
NRSVCE.10:
A capable wife who can find?
She is far more precious than jewels.
NRSVCE.11:
The heart of her husband trusts in her,
and he will have no lack of gain.
NRSVCE.12:
She does him good, and not harm,
all the days of her life.
NRSVCE.13:
She seeks wool and flax,
and works with willing hands.
NRSVCE.14:
She is like the ships of the merchant,
she brings her food from far away.
NRSVCE.15:
She rises while it is still night
and provides food for her household
and tasks for her servant-girls.
NRSVCE.16:
She considers a field and buys it;
with the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
NRSVCE.17:
She girds herself with strength,
and makes her arms strong.
NRSVCE.18:
She perceives that her merchandise is profitable.
Her lamp does not go out at night.
NRSVCE.19:
She puts her hands to the distaff,
and her hands hold the spindle.
NRSVCE.20:
She opens her hand to the poor,
and reaches out her hands to the needy.
NRSVCE.21:
She is not afraid for her household when it snows,
for all her household are clothed in crimson.
NRSVCE.22:
She makes herself coverings;
her clothing is fine linen and purple.
NRSVCE.23:
Her husband is known in the city gates,
taking his seat among the elders of the land.
NRSVCE.24:
She makes linen garments and sells them;
she supplies the merchant with sashes.
NRSVCE.25:
Strength and dignity are her clothing,
and she laughs at the time to come.
NRSVCE.26:
She opens her mouth with wisdom,
and the teaching of kindness is on her tongue.
NRSVCE.27:
She looks well to the ways of her household,
and does not eat the bread of idleness.
NRSVCE.28:
Her children rise up and call her happy;
her husband too, and he praises her:
NRSVCE.29:
“Many women have done excellently,
but you surpass them all.”
NRSVCE.30:
Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain,
but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.
NRSVCE.31:
Give her a share in the fruit of her hands,
and let her works praise her in the city gates.

NET.1: The words of King Lemuel, an oracle that his mother taught him:
NET.2: O my son, O son of my womb, O son of my vows,
NET.3: Do not give your strength to women, nor your ways to that which ruins kings.
NET.4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, or for rulers to crave strong drink,
NET.5: lest they drink and forget what is decreed, and remove from all the poor their legal rights.
NET.6: Give strong drink to the one who is perishing, and wine to those who are bitterly distressed;
NET.7: let them drink and forget their poverty, and remember their misery no more.
NET.8: Open your mouth on behalf of those unable to speak, for the legal rights of all the dying.
NET.9: Open your mouth, judge in righteousness, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
NET.10: Who can find a wife of noble character? For her value is far more than rubies.
NET.11: The heart of her husband has confidence in her, and he has no lack of gain.
NET.12: She brings him good and not evil all the days of her life.
NET.13: She obtains wool and flax, and she is pleased to work with her hands.
NET.14: She is like the merchant ships; she brings her food from afar.
NET.15: She also gets up while it is still night, and provides food for her household and a portion to her female servants.
NET.16: She considers a field and buys it; from her own income she plants a vineyard.
NET.17: She begins her work vigorously, and she strengthens her arms.
NET.18: She knows that her merchandise is good, and her lamp does not go out in the night.
NET.19: Her hands take hold of the distaff, and her hands grasp the spindle.
NET.20: She extends her hand to the poor, and reaches out her hand to the needy.
NET.21: She is not afraid of the snow for her household, for all of her household are clothed with scarlet.
NET.22: She makes for herself coverlets; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
NET.23: Her husband is well-known in the city gate when he sits with the elders of the land.
NET.24: She makes linen garments and sells them, and supplies the merchants with sashes.
NET.25: She is clothed with strength and honor, and she can laugh at the time to come.
NET.26: She opens her mouth with wisdom, and loving instruction is on her tongue.
NET.27: She watches over the ways of her household, and does not eat the bread of idleness.
NET.28: Her children rise up and call her blessed, her husband also praises her:
NET.29: “Many daughters have done valiantly, but you surpass them all!”
NET.30: Charm is deceitful and beauty is fleeting, but a woman who fears the LORD will be praised.
NET.31: Give her credit for what she has accomplished, and let her works praise her in the city gates.
